paper_metadata.title: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
paper_metadata.abstract: We present Sparrow, an information-seeking dialogue agent trained to be more helpful, correct, and harmless compared to prompted language model baselines. We use reinforcement learning from human feedback to train our models with two new additions to help human raters judge agent behaviour. First, to make our agent more helpful and harmless, we break down the requirements for good dialogue into natural language rules the agent should follow, and ask raters about each rule separately. We demonstrate that this breakdown enables us to collect more targeted human judgements of agent behaviour and allows for more efficient rule-conditional reward models. Second, our agent provides evidence from sources supporting factual claims when collecting preference judgements over model statements. For factual questions, evidence provided by Sparrow supports the sampled response 78% of the time. Sparrow is preferred more often than baselines while being more resilient to adversarial probing by humans, violating our rules only 8% of the time when probed. Finally, we conduct extensive analyses showing that though our model learns to follow our rules it can exhibit distributional biases.

extraction_data.innovation: Sparrow is a dialogue agent trained to avoid providing opinions on political, social, or religious issues, and to provide factual information while being polite, inclusive, and respectful. It is designed to handle sensitive topics without expressing personal opinions or biases.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (not specified in paper, inferred from context)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) with rule-based rewards
extraction_data.optimizer: Not specified (use null if not mentioned)
extraction_data.extension: Uses targeted human feedback to correct hallucinations and factual inaccuracies, enabling safer and more accurate dialogue agents.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving alignment of dialogue agents to avoid harmful stereotypes and ensure factual accuracy
extraction_data.application: Dialogue agents for factual question answering and avoiding harmful stereotypes
